question: What are the richest countries in the EU by GDP per capita?
What are the richest countries in South America by GDP per capita?
Suppose that an expansionary fiscal policy leads to a large increase in real output and a small increase in the price level. From this it can be inferred that
choices: inflation had already impacted the economy before the fiscal stimulus.
the economy initially had some unemployed resources.
aggregate supply decreased.
aggregate demand is steeply sloped.

question: When both short-run aggregate supply and aggregate demand increase which of the following can be said for certain?
choices: The price level rises but real GDP falls.
Both the price level and real GDP rise.
The price level rises but the change in real GDP is uncertain.
Real GDP rises but the change in the price level is uncertain.

question: Households demand more money as an asset when
choices: nominal GDP falls.
the nominal interest rate falls.
bond prices fall.
the supply of money falls.

question: When a nation is operating at the natural rate of unemployment
choices: there is no cyclical unemployment.
the inflation rate is zero.
there is no structural unemployment.
the nation is experiencing a recession.

question: Equilibrium real GDP is far below full employment and the government lowers household taxes. Which is the likely result?
choices: Unemployment falls with little inflation.
Unemployment rises with little inflation.
Unemployment falls with rampant inflation.
Unemployment rises with rampant inflation.

question: An increase in the consumer price index is commonly referred to as
choices: economic growth.
inflation.
unemployment.
discouraged workers.

question: The economy is currently operating at full employment. Assuming flexible wages and prices how would a decline in aggregate demand affect GDP and the price level in the short run and GDP and the price level in the long run? SHORT-RUN GDP     SHORT-RUN PRICE LEVEL     LONG-RUN GDP     LONG-RUN PRICE L
choices: Falls     Falls     No change     Falls
Falls     Falls     Falls     Falls
No change     Falls     No change     No change
Falls     Falls     No change     No change

question: The real interest rate is
choices: what one sees when looking at bank literature
the nominal interest rate divided by the inflation rate
the nominal interest rate plus the anticipated inflation rate
the nominal interest rate minus anticipated inflation

question: Potential GDP will fall ceteris paribus if
choices: the unemployment rate rises.
the retirement age is lowered.
tariffs protecting domestic jobs are eliminated.
more immigration is allowed.

question: Fisher's Hypothesis states that
choices: the real interest equals the nominal interest rate plus the inflation rate.
the nominal interest rate equals the real interest rate minus the inflation rate.
the nominal interest rate equals the unemployment rate plus the real interest rate.
the nominal interest rate equals the real interest rate plus the inflation rate.

question: An increase in the price of forklifts imported into the United States from Belgium will
choices: increase the consumer price index and the GDP deflator.
increase the consumer price index but not the GDP deflator.
increase the GDP deflator but not the consumer price index.
have no effect on the consumer price index or the GDP deflator.

question: National income measures
choices: household income in the nation.
income earned by the factors of production.
GDP minus depreciation and indirect business taxes.
(B) (C) and (D).

question: What does the presence of discouraged workers do to the measurement of the unemployment rate?
choices: Discouraged workers are counted as "out of the labor force" thus the unemployment rate is understated making the economy look stronger than it is.
Discouraged workers are counted as "out of the labor force" thus the unemployment rate is understated making the economy look weaker than it is.
Discouraged workers are not surveyed so there is no impact on the unemployment rate.
Discouraged workers are counted as "unemployed" thus the unemployment rate is understated making the economy look stronger than it is.

question: In the equation GDP = C + I + G + X X stands for
choices: exports.
expenditures.
exports minus imports.
imports minus exports.

question: The government measures inflation using the
choices: GNP
URL
CPI
FED

question: Operating in the intermediate range of the aggregate supply curve an increase in aggregate demand results in an increase in
choices: price level only
real GDP only
neither price level nor real GDP
price level and real GDP

question: If XYZ Corporation buys an original Matisse painting to hang in its board room then
choices: GDP decreases by the amount of the purchase because C decreases.
GDP increases by the amount of the purchase because I increases.
GDP is unaffected because it is a second hand sale.
GI)P decreases because I decreases.

question: It is unlikely that the unemployment rate will ever fall to zero because of
choices: frictional unemployment.
cyclical unemployment.
government policies.
corporate policies.

question: Which of the following shifts the production possibilities frontier outward?
choices: A decrease in the price level.
An increase in labor productivity.
An increase in the money supply.
A decrease in the unemployment rate.

question: Which of the following is true of money and financial markets?
choices: As the demand for bonds increases the interest rate increases.
For a given money supply if nominal GDP increases the velocity of money decreases.
When demand for stocks and bonds increases the asset demand for money falls.
A macroeconomic recession increases the demand for loanable funds.

question: Using the model of AD and AS what happens in the short run to real GDP the price level and unemployment with more consumption spending (C)? REAL GDP     PRICE LEVEL     UNEMPLOYMENT
choices: Increases     Decreases     Decreases
Decreases     Increases     Increases
Increases     Increases     Decreases
Decreases     Decreases     Decreases

question: Suppose the economy is operating beyond full employment. Which of the following is true at this point?
choices: The short-run aggregate supply curve is horizontal.
Further increases in aggregate demand will result in a lower price level.
A decrease in aggregate demand will result in a lower price level if prices are sticky.
Further increases in aggregate demand will not lower the unemployment rate but will create inflation.

question: An American buys an entertainment system that was manufactured in China. How does the U.S. national income accounts treat this transaction?
choices: Net exports and GDP both rise.
Net exports and GDP both fall.
Net exports and GDP go in opposite directions.
Net exports fall and there is no change in GDP.

question: If real GDP per capita was $20000 in 1980 and $21000 in 1990 then we conclude that the standard of living has increased
choices: 0.5 percent.
5.0 percent.
50 percent.
3.3 percent.

question: If the inflation rate is expected to increase in the immediate future then
choices: consumers will begin saving more now.
the velocity of money will fall.
this will put upward pressure on the nominal interest rate.
this will put downward pressure on the real interest rate.

question: If the economy were experiencing a recessionary gap choose the option below that would be an appropriate fiscal policy to eliminate the gap and the predicted impact of the policy on real GDP and unemployment. FISCAL POLICY     REAL GDP     UNEMPLOYMENT
choices: Increase taxes.     Increase     Decrease
Decrease spending.     Decrease     Increase
Decrease taxes.     Increase     Increase
Decrease taxes.     Increase     Decrease

question: Suppose that Tiger Woods buys a golf ball in England for $1 and the marginal propensity to consume in England is 0.75. What is the total increase in England's real GDP resulting from Mr. Woods' purchase?
If Real GDP = $200 billion and the price index = 200 Nominal GDP is
choices: $4 billion
$400 billion
$200 billion
$2 billion

question: To deflate nominal gross domestic product (GDP) you must
choices: divide nominal GDP by the GDP deflator.
multiply real GDP by the GDP deflator.
divide real GDP by the GDP deflator.
multiply nominal GDP by the GDP deflator.

question: To move the economy closer to full employment the central bank decides that the federal funds rate must be increased. The appropriate open market operation is to ______ which ______ the money supply ______ aggregate demand and fights ______. OMO     MONEY SUPPLY     AD     TO FIGHT
choices: Buy bonds     Increases     Increases     Unemployment
Buy bonds     Increases     Increases     Inflation
Sell bonds     Decreases     Decreases     Unemployment
Sell bonds     Decreases     Decreases     Inflation

question: U.S. real GDP most likely falls when
choices: tariffs and quotas are removed.
investment in human capital is high.
the money supply is increased.
the value of the dollar relative to foreign currencies is high.

question: Investment demand most likely increases when
choices: real GDP decreases.
the cost of acquiring and maintaining capital equipment rises.
investor optimism improves.
the real rate of interest rises.

question: A change in government spending will have a greater short run impact on real output when
choices: the marginal propensity to consume is lower.
the velocity of money is lower.
the velocity of money is higher.
the marginal propensity to consume is larger.

question: An American firm moves a manufacturing plant from the United States to Brazil. How will this affect gross domestic product (GDP) in the United States and in Brazil?
choices: U.S. GDP falls and Brazil's GDP falls.
U.S. GDP rises and Brazil's GDP falls.
U.S. GDP falls and Brazil's GDP remains constant.
U.S. GDP falls and Brazil's GDP rises.

question: Which of the following best measures changes in the price level of national product?
choices: The consumer price index
The real interest rate
The unemployment rate
The GDP deflator

question: When a large increase in aggregate demand has an even greater increase in real GDP economists refer to this as
choices: the balanced budget multiplier.
the money multiplier.
the foreign substitution effect.
the spending multiplier.

question: Which of the following would be included in U.S. GDP calculations?
choices: An auto mechanic fixing his dentist’s car for a filling
A stay-at-home dad providing childcare for his children
A worker donating $200 to the Red Cross
High school students spending their Saturdays building homes for the homeless

question: Expansionary monetary policy is designed to
choices: decrease the interest rate increase private investment increase aggregate demand and increase domestic output.
decrease the interest rate increase private investment increase aggregate demand and increase the unemployment rate.
increase the interest rate increase private investment increase aggregate demand and increase domestic output.
increase the interest rate decrease private investment increase aggregate demand and increase domestic output.

question: If the cost of the basket of goods and services the Bureau of Labor Statistics uses to calculate the consumer price index rises from $200 in the base period to $400 in the current period then the consumer price index
choices: equals 0.5 in the current period.
has risen 5 percent from the base to the current period.
equals 50 in the current period.
equals 200 in the current period.

question: Which of the following would NOT shift the aggregate demand curve?
choices: A change in consumer confidence.
A change in technology.
A change in the money supply.
A change in spending by state governments.

question: If real GDP per capita grows at a rate of 10 percent a year then we can expect the standard of living to double in
choices: 10 years.
9 years.
8 years.
7 years.

question: Which of the following would shift the aggregate demand curve to the left?
choices: An increase in consumer confidence.
Business firms expect lower sales in the future.
Foreigners develop a preference for our products.
Government increases its level of spending.

question: Suppose the President plans to cut taxes for consumers and also plans to increase spending on the military. How does this affect real GDP and the price level?
choices: GDP increases and the price level decreases.
GDP decreases and the price level increases.
GDP stays the same and the price level increases.
GDP increases and the price level increases.

question: Which of the following choices is most likely to create the greatest decrease in real GDP?
choices: The government decreases spending matched with a decrease in taxes.
The government increases spending with no increase in taxes.
The government decreases spending with no change in taxes.
The government holds spending constant while increasing taxes.

question: The CPI is calculated for each by
choices: week; The Bureau of Economic Analysis
month; The Bureau of Economic Analysis
month; The Bureau of Labor Statistics
quarter; The Bureau of Economic Analysis

question: What will happen to the equilibrium price level and the equilibrium quantity of output if consumer confidence increases? Assume an upward sloping aggregate supply curve.
choices: The equilibrium price level increases while the equilibrium quantity of output decreases.
The equilibrium price level decreases while the equilibrium quantity of output increases.
The equilibrium price level and quantity of output increase.
The equilibrium price level and quantity of output decrease.

question: You buy 100 shares in XYZ Corporation on the Internet and your broker charges you $29.95.
choices: This will increase the investment component of GDP and therefore overall GDP.
This has no effect on GDP.
This will increase GDP by $29.95.
This will increase GDP by the cost of the shares minus $29.95.

question: Which of the following is a predictable advantage of expansionary monetary policy in a recession?
choices: Decreases aggregate demand so that the price level falls.
Increases aggregate demand which increases real GDP and increases employment.
Increases unemployment but low prices negate this effect.
It keeps interest rates high which attracts foreign investment.

question: If the economy is experiencing a recession how will a plan to decrease taxes for consumers and increase spending on government purchases affect real gross domestic product (GDP) and the price level?
choices: real GDP rises and the price level falls.
real GDP falls and the price level rises.
real GDP rises and the price level rises.
real GDP falls and the price level falls.

question: Long-run aggregate supply is most likely to increase as the result of
choices: an increase in the real interest rate
increased investment in capital
an increase in aggregate demand
an increase in the unemployment rate

question: Which of the following statements is true?
choices: The velocity of money is equal to real GDP divided by the money supply.
Dollars earned today have more purchasing power than dollars earned a year from today.
The supply of loanable funds consists of investors.
The demand for loanable funds consists of savers.

question: If the economy experienced a decrease in real GDP and price level this could best be explained by
choices: a decline in labor productivity.
a technological advance.
a decline in investment.
an uptick in net exports.

question: If the consumer price index (CPI) increases by 2 percent and your nominal income increases by 8 percent your real income has approximately
choices: increased by 4 percent.
decreased by 4 percent.
increased by 6 percent.
decreased by 6 percent.

question: The standard of living is measured by
choices: GDP.
GDP per capita.
Real GDP per capita.
Actual GI)P per capita.

question: If the marginal propensity to consume is 0.8 what is the largest total increase in GDP that can result from $500 of new spending?

choices: An increase in aggregate demand.
A decrease in both aggregate demand and aggregate supply.
An increase in both aggregate demand and aggregate supply.
An increase in aggregate supply.

question: If prices are expected to rise more slowly in the future
choices: the Phillips curve will shift to the right
the actual rate of inflation will increase
the actual inflation rate will remain steady unless supply shocks set in
these expectations will become reality

question: When products improve in quality the CPI will
choices: automatically increase.
automatically decrease.
become negative.
overestimate the inflation rate.

question: Which of the following is true when expansionary fiscal and expansionary monetary (easy money) policy are used at the same time?
choices: Unemployment will increase.
Real GDP will decrease.
Interest rates will remain relatively constant.
Inflation will decrease dramatically.

question: Suppose real GDP increases. We can conclude without a doubt that
choices: prices are higher.
employment is higher.
production is higher.
prices and/or output is higher.

question: If nominal GDP equals $5000 and real GDP equals $4000 then the GDP Deflator equals
According to the quantity theory of money increasing the money supply serves to
choices: stimulate short-run production and employment with very little long-run inflation.
increase short-run output but it is the source of long-run inflation.
lower the unemployment rate while also lowering the rate of inflation.
increase the nation's long-run capacity to produce.

question: Real GDP
choices: is actual GDP as opposed to the estimate made by the BEA.
is also called current-dollar GDP.
is GDP adjusted for price changes.
is not calculated for the United States.

question: The "crowding-out" effect is the result of
choices: decreasing interest rates from contractionary fiscal policy.
increasing interest rates from expansionary fiscal policy.
increasing interest rates from expansionary monetary policy.
increasing unemployment rates from expansionary monetary policy.

question: The cabbages you grow in your summer garden are
choices: counted in GDP under C.
counted in GDP under I.
counted in GDP but not NDP.
not counted in GDP.

question: If a nation is operating at full employment and the central bank engages in contractionary monetary policy the nation can expect the interest rate the purchases of new homes and the unemployment rate to change in which of the following ways? INTEREST RATES     NEW HOMES     UNEMPLOYMENT RATE
choices: Decrease     Increase     Increase
Decrease     Decrease     Decrease
Increase     Decrease     Decrease
Increase     Decrease     Increase

question: Which of the following statements would "supply-side" economists disagree with?
choices: Tax changes cause shifts in aggregate supply that work against shifts in aggregate demand thus reducing the effect of the tax change on real GDP.
A tax cut is likely to increase aggregate supply by boosting saving investment and thus capital accumulation.
A tax increase is likely to decrease aggregate supply by decreasing after-tax wages and thus providing disincentives to work.
A tax cut is likely to increase aggregate supply by providing greater rewards for risk taking.

question: If real GDP per capita was $10000 in 1990 and $15000 in 2000 then the amount of economic growth is
choices: 0.5 percent.
5.0 percent.
50 percent.
3.3 percent.

question: If the government of country Z increases spending by $12 million dollars and raises tax collections by the same amount then what will be the overall impact of these moves on real GDP in country Z?
choices: Real GDP will increase by $6 million.
Real GDP will decrease by $6 million.
Real GDP will remain unchanged.
Real GDP will increase by $12 million.

question: Suppose the price level in the United States has risen in the past year but production of goods and services has remained constant. Based on this information which of the following is true? NOMINAL GDP     REAL GDP
choices: Increased     Increased
No change     Decreased
Decreased     Decreased
Increased     Decreased

question: Imagine an economy that produces only two goods cheese and crackers. Calculate GDP for this economy if cheese retails for $3 a pound and 10 pounds are produced while crackers sell for $2 a pound and 20 pounds are produced.

An increase in taxes

question: If current real GDP is $5000 and full employment real GDP is at $4000 which of the following combinations of policies is the most likely to have brought the economy to this point?
choices: A decrease in taxes and a lower discount rate
An increase in government spending and an increase in taxes
A decrease in taxes and selling bonds in an open market operation

question: According to experts the CPI
choices: overstates increases in the cost of living.
understates increases in the cost of living.
accurately estimates changes in the cost of living.

If 200 computers with a retail value of $100000 are domestically produced in 2005 but not sold until 2006 then GDP in 2005 is

200 higher because of the computers.
unaffected until 2006 when the computers are sold and the figure for GDP in 2005 is revised.
higher by the wholesale value of the computers.

choices: add depreciation expenses
add indirect taxes
subtract subsidies
all of the above

question: "Flash" estimates of GDP

do not require revision.
are available after a thirty-day lag.

question: If the CPI goes to 150 from 120 then prices have

question: The short-run Phillips curve depicts the ____ relationship between ____ and ____.

the central bank reserves the right to audit any bank at any time

question: The government has just lowered personal income taxes. Which of the following best describes the effects that this policy has on the economy?

Higher disposable income lower consumption higher real GDP lower unemployment
Lower disposable income higher consumption higher real GDP lower unemployment

question: Suppose you observe an economy where prices are falling and real GDP is rising. This may have been caused by
choices: stagflation.
an advance in technology.
an increase in government spending.

question: Suppose taxes are cut in an economy that is in equilibrium at full employment. In the long run the tax cut will
choices: raise real output and raise the price level.
lower real output and raise the price level.
raise real output and lower the price level.

question: Which choice produces a faster rate of economic growth for the United States?
choices: Institution of higher tariffs on imported goods
More investment in capital infrastructure and less consumption of nondurable goods and services

question: An increase in the price level and a decrease in real GDP in the short run could be the result of
choices: a prolonged period of very bad weather.
a decrease in the money supply.
a decrease in consumer confidence.

question: Which of the following fiscal policy combinations would be most likely to slowly increase real GDP without putting tremendous upward pressure on the price level?
choices: Increase government spending with a matching decrease in taxes.
Decrease government spending with a matching increase in taxes.
Increase government spending with no change in taxes.

choices: increase as consumers demand more money as a financial asset increasing the interest rate.
increase as consumers demand more money for transactions increasing the interest rate.
decrease as the purchasing power of the dollar is falling decreasing the interest rate.

question: Which of the following will result in economic growth?

choices: Stock price will likely decrease.

This allows us to determine how the quantity of an offering relates to the price at which it is offered:

question: These goods are bought infrequently, used repeatedly, and involve a reasonably high level of consumer risk:
